sn9.9:1.1: On one occasion a certain monk, a Vajjian princeling, was staying near Vesālī in a forest grove.
sn9.9:1.2: And on that occasion an all-night festival was being held in Vesālī.
sn9.9:1.3: The monk—lamenting as he heard the resounding din of wind music, string music, & gongs coming from Vesālī, on that occasion recited this verse:
sn9.9:2.1: “I live in the wilderness all alone
sn9.9:2.2: like a log cast away in the forest.
sn9.9:2.4: who could there be more miserable than me?”
sn9.9:3.1: Then the devatā inhabiting the forest grove, feeling sympathy for the monk, desiring his benefit, desiring to bring him to his senses, approached him and addressed him with this verse:
sn9.9:4.1: “As you live in the wilderness all alone
sn9.9:4.2: like a log cast away in the forest,
sn9.9:4.3: many are those who envy you,
sn9.9:4.4: as hell-beings do, those headed for heaven.”
sn9.9:5.1: The monk, chastened by the devatā, came to his senses.
